query: El Nino forecast development 2026, current ENSO status June 2026
answer:
Good — the first agent retrieved extensive data from authoritative sources. I now have enough to compile a comprehensive report.

---

# ENSO Status & El Niño Forecast Development — June 2026

## Executive Summary

**El Niño conditions are officially present** as of June 2026. The NOAA Climate Prediction Center (CPC) issued an **El Niño Advisory** on June 11, 2026, indicating observed El Niño conditions expected to continue and strengthen. The Niño 3.4 index has surged to **+1.5°C** (week of June 10, 2026), well above the +0.5°C El Niño threshold. Forecasters project a **63% chance of a very strong El Niño during November–January 2026–27**, potentially ranking among the largest El Niño events in the historical record since 1950.

---

## 1. Current ENSO Status

- **Official Status:** El Niño Advisory (CPC, issued June 11, 2026)
- **Synopsis:** "El Niño conditions are present and expected to strengthen into the Northern Hemisphere winter 2026-27."
- The El Niño Advisory is the highest active alert level in the CPC ENSO Alert System, meaning El Niño conditions are both observed and expected to persist.

## 2. Sea Surface Temperature Anomalies

### Weekly SST Data (Most Recent)

| Week Centered On | Niño 1+2 SSTA | Niño 3 SSTA | Niño 3.4 SSTA | Niño 4 SSTA |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| May 27, 2026 | +2.2°C | +1.3°C | +1.0°C | +1.1°C |
| Jun 3, 2026 | +2.6°C | +1.5°C | +1.3°C | +1.1°C |
| **Jun 10, 2026** | **+2.7°C** | **+1.6°C** | **+1.5°C** | **+1.3°C** |

The Niño 3.4 index rose from +0.9°C in mid-May to **+1.5°C by June 10** — a rapid intensification. The eastern Pacific (Niño 1+2) shows especially strong warming at +2.7°C, indicating an eastern Pacific (EP) El Niño signature.

### Monthly SST Anomalies (2026 Progression)

| Month | Niño 1+2 | Niño 3 | Niño 3.4 | Niño 4 |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Jan 2026 | -0.29 | -0.50 | -0.54 | -0.04 |
| Feb 2026 | +0.92 | -0.01 | -0.20 | +0.23 |
| Mar 2026 | +1.29 | +0.24 | +0.03 | +0.34 |
| Apr 2026 | +1.52 | +0.55 | +0.47 | +0.82 |
| May 2026 | +1.81 | +1.14 | **+0.94** | +0.98 |

### Oceanic Niño Index (ONI) — 3-Month Running Means

| Season | ONI Anomaly | Status |
|---|---|---|
| OND 2025 | **-0.55** | Weak La Niña |
| NDJ 2025-26 | -0.54 | Weak La Niña |
| DJF 2026 | -0.37 | Transitioning |
| JFM 2026 | -0.14 | Near-neutral |
| FMA 2026 | +0.13 | Neutral |
| MAM 2026 | **+0.48** | Near El Niño threshold |

This documents a remarkably rapid transition from **weak La Niña (late 2025) → neutral (early 2026) → El Niño (spring 2026)**.

## 3. Forecast Probabilities

### CPC Forecast (June 11, 2026)
- El Niño expected to **strengthen into NH winter 2026-27**
- **63% probability of a very strong El Niño during November–January 2026-27**
- A very strong event "would rank among the largest El Niño events in the historical record going back to 1950"
- High confidence driven by: anomalously high oceanic heat content, expanding westerly wind anomalies across the equatorial Pacific

### IRI/CPC Probabilistic Forecast (May 19, 2026)

| Season | El Niño Prob. | Neutral Prob. | La Niña Prob. |
|---|---|---|---|
| May–Jul 2026 | **98%** | 2% | ~0% |
| Jun–Aug 2026 | **~97–98%** | ~2–3% | ~0% |
| Jul–Sep 2026 through Jan–Mar 2027 | **97–98%** | 2–3% | ~0% |

These are remarkably high and consistent probabilities, reflecting exceptional confidence in El Niño persistence through at least early 2027.

## 4. Key Physical Drivers

- **Subsurface heat content** is exceptionally elevated — **more than twice the values observed during the same period in mid-May 2023** (during the 2023 El Niño event)
- Subsurface temperatures at 50–150m depth between 150°W and 80°W show anomalies **locally reaching up to 6°C**
- The **Southern Oscillation Index (SOI)** fell to -11.2 in April 2026, remaining strongly negative at -11.1 through mid-May
- Expanding **westerly wind anomalies** across the equatorial Pacific are reinforcing the warming pattern
- The coupled ocean-atmosphere system shows full El Niño engagement (low-level westerlies and upper-level easterlies over central equatorial Pacific)

## 5. Indian Ocean Dipole (IOD) Connection

- Current IOD is neutral (DMI = +0.31°C in April 2026)
- Models project **positive IOD conditions from July 2026 onward**, with probability exceeding 80% by September 2026
- A positive IOD concurrent with El Niño is a common and climatologically significant co-occurrence pattern that can amplify global teleconnections

## 6. Transition Timeline

| Period | Event |
|---|---|
| OND 2025 | Weak La Niña (ONI = -0.55) |
| DJF 2026 | Transition toward neutral (ONI = -0.37) |
| FMA 2026 | Neutral (ONI = +0.13) |
| MAM 2026 | Near El Niño threshold (ONI = +0.48) |
| May 2026 | Monthly Niño 3.4 = +0.94°C |
| Jun 10, 2026 | Weekly Niño 3.4 = **+1.5°C** |
| Jun 11, 2026 | CPC issues **El Niño Advisory** |
| Nov–Jan 2026-27 | **63% chance of very strong El Niño** |

## 7. Caveats

- The IRI notes forecasts are being issued near the end of the **boreal spring predictability barrier**, historically associated with lower ENSO forecast skill
- Even very strong El Niño events "do not lead to the expected impact everywhere," though stronger events more significantly tilt the odds toward expected outcomes
- Next CPC ENSO Diagnostic Discussion scheduled for **July 9, 2026**

---
